accurate.why: All claims accurately reflect the bill text; OIRA identification and procedural details are correct.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as expanding RFA requirements and creating a petition review process for small business regulatory impacts.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Accurately describes amendments to sections 603, 605, 605A, 609, and 610 of title 5.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the 'where feasible' condition, petition content requirements, and limits on Chief Counsel assistance.

effective_dates.why: Notes the 5-year lookback for Section 610 amendments and House passage on December 5, 2024.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers all major sections: indirect cost analysis, 10-day certification publication, petition process, guidance publication, periodic review reforms.

coherent.why: Well-organized with logical flow through each section.

concise.why: Comprehensive but not padded; length reflects the bill's complexity.

correct_entities.why: SBA Chief Counsel for Advocacy, OIRA Administrator, regulations.gov all correctly identified.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
